title: Experimental 20-second Greek ignition and harvest-rotation prior
interpretation: On one 20-second session, relative Greek combinations were more informative than isolated GEX. A put-ignition candidate appeared when SPX net DEX was weak relative to SPY while the SPX-versus-SPY GEX-ratio gap and relative put-versus-call IV skew were elevated. A separate fast-call candidate appeared when SPY net DEX and SPX net VEX were elevated. Treat these as early-warning hypotheses for cheap-contract expansion, not automatic entries. Directional structure must appear before broad repricing, the selected contract must still have executable runway, and same-side chain breadth should be beginning rather than already saturated. After a broad current-side burst, a sharp collapse in relative DEX and charm together with expanding relative vanna and first activation of opposite-side breadth may indicate exhaustion and possible rotation. One observed rotation is insufficient to establish a rule.
usePolicy: Experimental low-confidence prior only. Never apply or reveal this memory during replay of 2026-07-15 because it was derived from that full session. On later unseen dates, use it only to generate questions and modestly adjust attention. It may not trigger BUY_CALL, BUY_PUT, continuation, full-port re-entry, or side rotation by itself. Require fresh pre-expansion price transmission, acceptable spread and liquidity, remaining structural runway, and early same-side breadth. Do not chase after the contract or neighboring strikes have already delivered the intended expansion. Treat +40% as the primary ultra-short research benchmark; do not assume 40-to-60-second doubles are routine. Record confirmations and contradictions separately and lower or retire the memory if independent days fail to reproduce it.
waitingRoomReason: References DEX/VEX inputs no longer available to the live simulator; preserved for future offline research only.
